critique.works_well: The visual identity is genuinely distinctive — the checkerboard grid with lifestyle photography is bold, editorial, and communicates consumer lifestyle brand ambition with real design taste. Typography is confident and the off-white palette with high-contrast black feels intentional and premium, not templated. This would not look out of place next to a well-funded consumer app brand.
critique.looks_weak: The entire website is a single screen with zero scroll content — no features, no how-it-works, no team, no social proof of any kind. The headline 'Your (non-work) life, wrangled.' is clever but leaves the actual product completely opaque; the mysterious numbers in the grid (4, 6, 10, 14, 16, 18, 20, 24, 26, 28) are unexplained and don't help. The only CTA is 'Download app to join waitlist →' which signals pre-launch with no traction.
critique.single_biggest_improvement: Add a one-line product descriptor that explains what the app actually does (e.g. 'The app that organizes your social life, hobbies, and personal time') — a visitor should never have to guess what they're signing up for.

notes: Full-page screenshot confirmed: the hero IS the entire website. No scroll content exists. Scores revised downward on visual_hierarchy, trust_signals, cta_clarity, and investor_signal to reflect this.
